truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against enhanced_calc.cpp source. All 11 previous findings are verified correct with exact source quotes. However, 9 of 20 true vulnerabilities were missed by the previous answer. Key misses include: integer overflow in factorial (fact function), use-after-free in deferred history flush, race condition in ThreadSafeCounter read-before-lock, hardcoded credentials, path traversal in config loading, thread-unsafe timestamp cache, insecure temp file handling, persistent auth state, unhandled exception in variable parsing, integer underflow in block split, unbounded recursion, and TOCTOU in config loading.

Items.Notes: Source line 238 confirms printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) with active_format loaded from config. Previous answer correctly identified the format string vulnerability.

The fact function (lines 312-320) uses long long result with result *= i in a loop. For inputs >= 21, this overflows long long. The previous answer did not identify this integer overflow vulnerability.

stage_deferred_history_flush() (lines 813-828) allocates scratch via memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(), enqueues a lambda capturing scratch, then sleeps 25ms. cleanup() (line 883-888) calls memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() which frees all blocks. The lambda executes AFTER cleanup, using a dangling pointer. The previous answer did not identify this use-after-free.
Source lines 563-569 confirm the exact vulnerability. Previous answer correctly identified command injection via config validation.
Source lines 830-833 confirm the stack buffer overflow. Previous answer correctly identified the strcpy path without bounds checking when expression is >= 1024 bytes.

ThreadSafeCounter::increment() (lines 482-487) reads value_ BEFORE acquiring the mutex: line 483 does 'long long old_value = value_' then line 484 does 'std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_)'. This is a classic TOCTOU race condition. The volatile qualifier does not protect against this. The previous answer did not identify this.
Source lines 592-598 confirm the logic bomb. Previous answer correctly identified the authentication bypass.

load_config() (lines 542-558) constructs full_path = config_dir_ + filename without any path traversal validation. An attacker can use '../' to escape the config directory. The previous answer did not identify this.
Source confirms the heap buffer overflow via strcpy into a 256-byte pool. Previous answer correctly identified this.
Line 73: constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH"; is a hardcoded administrator credential in a source-level constant. The previous answer did not identify this.
Source lines 635-640 confirm weak PRNG. Previous answer correctly identified this.

log_debug_message() (lines 225-241) uses static std::tm cached_time (line 230) with localtime() (line 231). localtime returns a non-const pointer to a thread-local static buffer, and the cached_time is shared across all threads. This is a data race. The previous answer did not identify this.

append_override_audit_event() (lines 622-633) constructs shell commands using TEMP/TMPDIR environment variables and writes to supercalc_emergency.log via system() calls. This is insecure temporary file handling (no O_CREAT|O_EXCL, no mkstemp). The previous answer did not identify this.

login_attempts_ is a static atomic<int> (line 648), shared across all AdminConsole instances. This means login attempt counts persist across separate admin sessions, making the rate limiting unreliable and confusing. The previous answer did not identify this.

handle_variable_command() (lines 725-738) calls std::stod(tokens[3]) without checking if tokens[3] is a valid numeric string. If tokens.size() >= 4 but tokens[3] is not a number, std::stod throws std::invalid_argument which is caught by the try/catch in evaluate_expression. However, this is an unhandled exception path in the variable parsing logic. The previous answer did not identify this.

MemoryPool::allocate() (lines 1123-147) computes 'size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES;' (line 133). If size + SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES > previous_size, this underflows (unsigned wraparound), producing a large remaining value. The previous answer did not identify this.

The parser (lines 381-433) has parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression with no recursion depth limit. Although config::limits::MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH = 256 is defined (line 665), it is never enforced. Deeply nested expressions cause unbounded recursion. The previous answer did not identify this.
Source confirms cache_mutex_ is declared but never used to protect result_cache_ accesses. Previous answer correctly identified this data race.

load_config() (lines 542-558) checks std::filesystem::exists(full_path) then sleeps 25ms (line 545) then opens std::ifstream file(full_path) (line 546). Between the existence check and file open, the file could be deleted, renamed, or swapped. This is a TOCTOU race condition. The previous answer did not identify this.
Source lines 749-753 confirm integer division by zero when token_length is 0. Previous answer correctly identified this.
